rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type contracts in the component type annex and implementation-level bindings in the implementation annex
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions, avoiding subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Align AGREE variable types with their expressions to avoid type mismatches
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as typed ports or constants
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL structure when inserting annex blocks
Use appropriate annex placement for assumptions, guarantees, and bindings
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the current scope
Avoid using AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions without proper exposure
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when possible
anti_patterns: Do not place subcomponent port references in component type contracts
Avoid using AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions without proper exposure
Do not mix component type and implementation annex content without valid scope
